perturbation.original_text: Registration Number: 2008/021152/07 (hereinafter “EnviroServ ”) and ………………………………………………………….. Registration Number: …./……/.. (hereinafter “Prospect ive Buyer ”) together ( with their subsidiaries, holding companies and fellow subsidiaries) shall comprise the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Registration Number: 2008/021152/07 (hereinafter “EnviroServ ”) and ………………………………………………………….. Registration Number: …./……/.. (hereinafter “Prospect ive Buyer ”) together ( with their subsidiaries, holding companies and fellow subsidiaries) shall comprise the Individuals.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Parties' to 'Individuals' introduces a contradiction. Throughout the contract, 'Parties' is used to define the entities bound by the agreement. Changing it to 'Individuals' creates uncertainty as to whether the agreement applies to the corporate entities (subsidiaries, holding companies) mentioned. This change generates an In-Text contradiction by not adhering to what was established previously by the contract. Now the contract refers to EnviroServ and Prospective buyer as Individuals, when they are companies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that they will not, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the information to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Parties' as comprising 'EnviroServ' and 'Prospective Buyer' along with their subsidiaries, holding companies, and fellow subsidiaries. The changed text alters 'Parties' to 'Individuals.' The contradicted text references 'parties,' implying corporate entities. By changing 'Parties' to 'Individuals,' the contract now refers to these entities as individuals, creating a contradiction because they are explicitly described as companies and related corporate entities (subsidiaries, holding companies). This introduces an In-Text contradiction by not adhering to what was established previously by the contract. The modified text introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with the contradicted section, making it unclear whether the agreement applies to the corporate entities.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this agreement, the party disclosing , such information shall be referred to as "the disclosing party" and the party receiving such information shall be referred to as "the receiving party".
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this agreement, the individual disclosing such information shall be referred to as "the disclosing individual" and the entity receiving such information shall be referred to as "the receiving entity".
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'disclosing party' to 'disclosing individual' and 'receiving party' to 'receiving entity' creates confusion, especially given the initial definition of 'Parties' and the fact that the agreement is between companies. This will have a negative impact because this change, has in-text implications. Now the contract refers to EnviroServ and Prospective buyer as Individuals, when they are companies. 'Entity' and 'Individual' are not used interchangeably.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information disclosed by the disclosing party to the receiving party is acknowledged by the receiving party: 4.1 to be proprietary to the disclosing party; and 4 4.2 not to confer any rights of whatever nature in such information to the receiving party.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the disclosing and receiving parties. The changed text alters these definitions to 'disclosing individual' and 'receiving entity'. The contradicted text refers to 'disclosing party' and 'receiving party'. Because the 'party' is now not an interchangeable word for individual/entity, this creates a strong contradiction and ambiguity. The agreement is between companies, so the changed text's reference to individuals conflicts with the rest of the contract. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. THE INFORMATION "Information" shall for the purpose of this agreement include, without limitation, any technical, commercial, scientific information, know -how, trade secrets, processes, machinery, designs, drawings, technical spe cifications, data in whatever form and information referred to in clause 1 (introduction) hereinabove communicated to the receiving party or acquired by the receiving party from the disclosing party during the course of the parties' association with one an other.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. THE DATA "Data" shall for the purpose of this agreement include, without limitation, any technical, commercial, scientific information, know -how, trade secrets, processes, machinery, designs, drawings, technical spe cifications, data in whatever form and information referred to in clause 1 (introduction) hereinabove communicated to the receiving entity or acquired by the receiving entity from the disclosing individual during the course of the Individuals' association with one an other.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Information' to 'Data', creates uncertainty. While 'data' can be a subset of 'information', the original agreement defines 'Information' broadly. Now it is not clear if things like 'know-how' are considered 'data'. This creates a contradiction with the rest of the agreement that refers to information and data as if they are the same thing. Also, 'receiving party' was changed to 'receiving entity' and 'disclosing party' was changed to 'disclosing individual' and 'Parties' to 'Individuals' to keep the contradiction going in-text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree to disclose the information to one another.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Information" as "Data" and replaces "party" with "entity/individual." The contradicted text states "The parties agree to disclose the information to one another." If 'Data' is not equivalent to 'Information' (which is plausible, as 'data' can be seen as a subset of 'information'), then the parties are not agreeing to disclose all information, which directly contradicts the original intention, and also the parties have been changed to individuals/entities. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 The parties agree that they will no t, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the information to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 The Individuals agree that they will no t, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the Data to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing individual, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Using 'Individuals', 'Data', and 'disclosing individual' instead of 'parties', 'information', and 'disclosing party' creates an inconsistency, generating an in-text contradiction. It's not clear if this clause now only applies to the individual representatives of the companies or to the companies themselves. This contradicts the initial scope of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: not to utilise, employ, exploit or in any other manner whatsoever use the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreem ent for any purpose without the prior express written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'parties' disclosing 'information', whereas the changed text refers to 'Individuals' disclosing 'Data'. The contradicted text refers to 'disclosing party' using 'information'. The change from 'parties' to 'Individuals' and 'information' to 'Data', and 'disclosing party' to 'disclosing individual' creates a contradiction because it is no longer clear if the restriction applies to the companies themselves or only their representatives, and whether the information is only Data or anything else. This is misaligned terminology in the form of an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. RESTRICTIONS ON DISCLOSURE AND USE OF THE INFORMATION 5.1 The receiving party may disclose the information only to its officers, employees and professional advisors and then only on a strictly need -to-know basis. 5.2 The receiving party undertakes not to use the information fo r purpose other than: 5.2.1 that for which it is disclosed; and 5.2.2 in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RESTRICTIONS ON DISCLOSURE AND USE OF THE DATA 5.1 The receiving entity may disclose the Data only to its officers, employees and professional advisors and then only on a strictly need -to-know basis. 5.2 The receiving entity undertakes not to use the Data fo r purpose other than: 5.2.1 that for which it is disclosed; and 5.2.2 in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'receiving party' to 'receiving entity', and 'information' to 'Data' creates contradiction in enforcement. The term 'information' and 'Data' are not defined in the same way. If a dispute arises, it will be open to interpretation as to whether the restriction applies to 'Data' or 'information'. This contradicts and creates uncertainty regarding the agreement's scope, because 'Information' was initially used and is a broader term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF INFORMATION 7.1 The disclo sing party may at any time, request the receiving party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to information disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and may, in addition request the receiving party to furnish a written stat ement to the effect that, upon such return, the receiving party has not retained in possession, or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such material. 7.2 As an alternative to the return of the material contemplated in 7.1 above, the receivin g party shall, at the instance of the disclosing party, destroy such material and furnish the disclosing party with a written statement to the effect that all such material has been destroyed. 5 7.3 The receiving party shall comply with a request, in terms of t his clause 7, within 7 days of receipt of such a request
perturbation.justification: The modification changes 'information' to 'Data' and 'receiving party' to 'receiving entity'. The contradicted text refers to 'information' which is being requested to be returned. Since 'Data' is not necessarily 'information' and 'receiving entity' is not necessarily 'receiving party', this creates a contradiction because the receiving party is obligated to return 'information', but the changed section only restricts the use of 'Data' by the 'receiving entity'. This means the 'receiving entity' may not be bound by the obligation to return 'information' under clause 7, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 7. RETURN OF INFORMATION 7.1 The disclo sing party may at any time, request the receiving party to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to information disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and may, in addition request the receiving party to furnish a written stat ement to the effect that, upon such return, the receiving party has not retained in possession, or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such material. 7.2 As an alternative to the return of the material contemplated in 7.1 above, the receivin g party shall, at the instance of the disclosing party, destroy such material and furnish the disclosing party with a written statement to the effect that all such material has been destroyed. 5 7.3 The receiving party shall comply with a request, in terms of t his clause 7, within 7 days of receipt of such a request,
perturbation.changed_text: 7. RETURN OF DATA 7.1 The disclosing individual may at any time, request the receiving entity to return any material containing, pertaining to or relating to Data disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and may, in addition request the receiving entity to furnish a written stat ement to the effect that, upon such return, the receiving entity has not retained in possession, or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such material. 7.2 As an alternative to the return of the material contemplated in 7.1 above, the receiving entity shall, at the instance of the disclosing individual, destroy such material and furnish the disclosing individual with a written statement to the effect that all such material has been destroyed. 5 7.3 The receiving entity shall comply with a request, in terms of t his clause 7, within 7 days of receipt of such a request,
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'RETURN OF INFORMATION' to 'RETURN OF DATA', 'disclosing party' to 'disclosing individual' and 'receiving party' to 'receiving entity' throughout the clause creates ambiguity and an In-Text contradiction. Given the previous changes, it is unclear if this section only applies to the specific 'Data' as defined earlier or if it extends to all 'Information' originally intended, and if this applies only to the Individual and the Entity. Because those terms doesn't reffered to the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. EXCLUDED INFORMATION The obligations of the parties pursuant to the provisions of this agreement shall not apply to any information that: 8.1 is known to or in possession of the receiving party prior to disclosure thereof by the disclosing party; 8.2 is or becomes publicly known, otherwise then pursuant to a breach of this agreement; 8.3 is developed independently of the disclosing party by the receiving party; 8.4 is disclosed by the receiving party to satisf y the order of a court of competent jurisdiction or to comply with the provisions of any law or regulation in force from time to time; provided that in these circumstances, the receiving party shall advise the disclosing party in writing prior to such disc losure to enable the disclosing party to take whatever steps it deems necessary to protect its interests in this regard, provided further that the receiving party will disclose only that portion of the information which it is legally required to disclose a nd the receiving party will use its reasonable endeavours to protect the confidentiality of such information to the widest extent possible in the circumstances; 8.5 is disclosed to a third party pursuant to the prior written authorization from the disclosing party; 8.6 is received from a third party in circumstances that do not result in a breach of the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'disclosing individual' and 'receiving entity' which potentially narrows the scope compared to 'disclosing party' and 'receiving party' in the original text. This creates ambiguity as to whether clause 7 now only applies to individuals and entities or still includes other 'parties' (e.g., organizations), as originally intended. This ambiguity directly contradicts clause 8, which uses the terms 'disclosing party' and 'receiving party' without qualification. If 'party' in clause 8 is interpreted more broadly than 'individual' or 'entity' in the changed clause 7, then the scope of excluded information in clause 8 may not apply consistently to the return of data under the modified clause 7, creating a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
